message:
feat: Add auto-parallelization for large list comprehensions

Analysis (function_traits.zig):
- analyzeListCompForParallel() detects parallelizable patterns
- isParallelizableExpr() ensures pure expressions (no side effects)
- Threshold: ≥1024 elements for parallel execution

Runtime (parallel.zig):
- parallelRangeMap() splits work across CPU cores
- parallelMapI64() for array transformations
- Automatic chunk sizing based on std.Thread.getCpuCount()

Codegen (comprehensions.zig):
- genParallelListComp() generates runtime.parallel calls
- Example: [x * 2 for x in range(10000)] → parallelRangeMap

Optimization tiers:
- <16 elements: Scalar loop
- 16-1023: SIMD vectorization (@Vector(4, i64))
- ≥1024: Multi-threaded parallel execution

This completes our "unique advantage" optimizations:
- Auto I/O↔CPU async switching
- Escape analysis
- Auto-SIMD
- Auto-parallelization

All impossible for interpreters, all require manual work in other languages.
